content.text:
Memory stage: NoteReconciler v1.
        Namespace: project:<0>

        Decide how candidate notes should update active note memory.

        Return JSON:
        {
          "operations": [
            {
              "action": "insert | noop | supersede | retract | update",
              "candidate_index": 0,
              "target_note_id": "existing-note-id-or-null",
              "patch": null,
              "links_to_create": [
                {
                  "to_note_id": "existing-note-id",
                  "link_type": "supports | contradicts | refines | related | supersedes | derived_from",
                  "link_weight": 1.0
                }
              ],
              "reason": "short explanation"
            }
          ]
        }

        Actions:
        - insert: candidate is new reusable context.
        - noop: candidate duplicates an existing active note or is lower value.
        - supersede: candidate is a better current version of target_note_id.
        - update: patch target_note_id without inserting a new note.
        - retract: target_note_id should no longer be active because TARGET_SOURCE retracts it.

        Rules:
        - Compare semantic meaning, not wording.
        - Do not keep duplicate notes just because titles differ.
        - Prefer updating or superseding an existing note when the candidate refines the same decision, rationale, plan, or procedure.
        - Insert when the candidate captures a distinct reusable semantic fragment.
        - Use noop for summary spam, weak paraphrases, or information already captured by an existing note.
        - Use links_to_create only for strong semantic relations between notes.
        - target_note_id must be one of Existing active notes below or null.
        - candidate_index is zero-based from Candidate notes below.
        - Return one operation per candidate unless TARGET_SOURCE only retracts or updates an existing note.
        - Return valid JSON only.

        TARGET_SOURCE:
        Document title: 01. Architecture
Document source: agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md
Document section: 01. Architecture / 17. Почему не надо начинать с graph DB
Document lines: 547-558

## 17. Почему не надо начинать с graph DB

`Entity + Claim + NoteLink + scope_json` уже дают graph-like semantics.
Для memory layer обычно хватает:

- Postgres
- JSONB
- pgvector
- full-text indexes
- lineage tables

Graph DB нужен, когда traversal/path queries становятся частью продукта, а не просто внутренним удобством.

        Candidate notes:
        [0] type=DOC_DIGEST title=Do not start v3 memory layer with a Graph DB summary=In `agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md`, section 17 argues that `Entity + Claim + NoteLink + scope_json` already provide graph-like semantics for the memory layer. The usual stack is `Postgres`, `JSONB`, `pgvector`, `full-text indexes`, and `lineage tables`; a Graph DB is only needed when traversal/path queries become a product feature rather than internal convenience. scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section '01. Architecture / 17.         Existing active notes:
        id=hot-path:note:<0> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=v3 memory architecture storage tiers summary=In `agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md`, the v3 architecture separates storage into five tiers: `Tier A. Core / in-context memory` for small prompt-ready blocks, `Tier B. Structured long-term memory` for typed memory objects, `Tier C. Rich note memory` for semantic notes and rationale, `Tier D. Raw evidence` for immutable source material, and `Tier E. Retrieval indexes` for vector, full-text,...[truncated 31 chars] scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section '01. Architecture / 5. Storage tiers' keywords=v3,storage tiers,Core / in-context memory,Structured long-term memory,Rich note memory,Raw evidence,Retrieval indexes,Postgres tags=doc_digest,architecture,storage_tiers useCount=0

id=hot-path:note:<5> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Responsibility split: runtime, memory manager, and storage layer summary=In `agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md`, section 10 assigns lightweight dialog, tool orchestration, read-time retrieval, and cheap routing/escalation to `User-facing runtime`; heavier routing, retrieval-before-update, canonicalization, extraction, reconciliation, consolidation, repair, and retention to `Memory worker / manager`; and immutable evidence, typed tables, transactional updates, lin...[truncated 112 chars] scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 10 'Разделение ответственности' keywords=User-facing runtime,Memory worker / manager,Storage layer,responsibility split,repair,retention,audit trail tags=memory_architecture,component_roles,responsibilities useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<6> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Optional fast/slow execution path with typed handoff objects summary=In `agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md`, section 16 proposes an optional post-MVP fast/slow execution split: `Fast path` is user-facing, lower-latency, with cheap retrieval, cheap draft, and an escalation gate; `Slow path` uses a stronger model/worker for conflict resolution, answer verification, note consolidation, and memory repair. Handoffs between them should use `typed handoff objects` i...[truncated 20 chars] scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section '01. Architecture / 16. Optional fast/slow execution path' keywords=Fast path,Slow path,typed handoff objects,lower latency,escalation gate,conflict resolution,answer verification,memory repair tags=doc_digest,memory_architecture,execution_path useCount=0

The minimum `scope_json` fields are `scope_kind`, `project_id`, `environment`, `subject_entity_id`, `conversation_id`, `modality`, and `audience`, so reconciliation is more predictable, retrieval overgenerali...[truncated 54 chars] scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section '01. responseFormat.description: Reconcile note candidates against active memory notes.
responseFormat.name: memory_note_reconciler

systemPrompts:
You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.
